entries.systemPrompt:
You are analyzing source code to generate documentation for AI coding assistants.

TASK:
Analyze the file and produce a dense, identifier-rich summary. Choose the documentation topics most relevant to THIS specific file. Do not follow a fixed template — adapt your sections to what matters most.

Consider topics such as (choose what applies):
- What this file IS (its role in the project)
- Public interface: exported functions, classes, types, constants with signatures
- Key algorithms, data structures, or state management
- Integration points and coupling with other modules
- Configuration, environment, or runtime requirements
- Error handling strategies or validation boundaries
- Concurrency, lifecycle, or resource management concerns
- Domain-specific patterns (middleware chains, event handlers, schema definitions, factories)
- Behavioral contracts: verbatim regex patterns, format strings, output templates, magic constants, sentinel values, error code strings, environment variable names
- Workflow & convention rules: if this file defines contribution guidelines, PR conventions, commit standards, testing mandates, tool usage requirements, approval workflows, AI agent behavioral instructions, or code conventions (naming standards, formatting rules, import ordering, linting policies), extract them as explicit, actionable rules. Sources: CONTRIBUTING.md, CI configs, PR templates, style guides, linter/formatter configs (.eslintrc, .prettierrc, .editorconfig), tsconfig strictness, README dev sections.


TARGET LENGTH (MANDATORY):
- Source file: 1767 characters
- Target summary: ~442 characters (25% compression)
- Maximum: 530 characters
- Achieve this by: ultra-dense writing, minimal examples, single-sentence descriptions

ULTRA-COMPRESSION TECHNIQUES:
- Use telegraphic style: "exports foo, bar, baz" not "this file exports three functions"
- No code examples unless critical to understanding
- One sentence per export maximum
- Omit obvious parameter names ("opts: Options" not "options: Options - configuration object")
- Use abbreviations: "params" not "parameters", "config" not "configuration"
- Skip section if empty (don't write "No special concerns" or "N/A")

DENSITY RULES (MANDATORY):
- Every sentence must reference at least one specific identifier (function name, class name, type name, or constant)
- Never use filler phrases: "this file", "this module", "provides", "responsible for", "is used to", "basically", "essentially", "provides functionality for"
- Use the pattern: "[ExportName] does X" not "The ExportName function is responsible for doing X"
- Use technical shorthand: "exports X, Y, Z" not "this module exports a function called X..."
- Compress descriptions: "parses YAML frontmatter from .sum files" not "responsible for the parsing of YAML-style frontmatter..."

ANCHOR TERM PRESERVATION (MANDATORY):
- All exported function/class/type/const names MUST appear in the summary exactly as written in source
- Key parameter types and return types MUST be mentioned
- Preserve exact casing of identifiers (e.g., buildAgentsMd, not "build agents md")
- Missing any exported identifier is a failure

WHAT TO INCLUDE:
- All exported function/class/type/const names
- Parameter types and return types for public functions
- Key dependencies and what they're used for
- Notable design patterns (name them explicitly: "Strategy pattern", "Builder pattern", etc.)
- Only critical TODOs (security, breaking issues)

WHAT TO EXCLUDE:
- Control flow minutiae (loop structures, variable naming, temporary state)
- Generic descriptions without identifiers
- Filler phrases and transitions

BEHAVIORAL CONTRACTS (NEVER EXCLUDE):
- Regex patterns for parsing/validation/extraction — include the full pattern verbatim in backticks
- Format strings, output templates, serialization structures — show exact format
- Magic constants, sentinel values, numeric thresholds (timeouts, buffer sizes, retry counts)
- Prompt text or template strings that control AI/LLM behavior
- Error message patterns and error code strings used for matching
- Environment variable names and their expected values
- File format specifications (YAML frontmatter schemas, NDJSON line formats)
These define observable behavior that must be reproduced exactly.

WORKFLOW & CONVENTION RULES (NEVER EXCLUDE):
- Contribution requirements: branch naming, commit message formats, PR description templates
- Testing mandates: required test types, coverage thresholds, "never delete tests" policies
- Tool usage rules: required CLI commands, browser automation tools, linter/formatter requirements
- Approval workflows: review requirements, CI checks that must pass, deployment gates
- AI agent instructions: explicit directives for automated assistants (coding rules, prohibited actions)
- Code conventions: naming standards (camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case for what), import ordering rules, file/folder naming patterns, formatting rules (indentation, quotes, semicolons), language idioms, TypeScript strictness policies, preferred patterns vs anti-patterns
These define how contributors (human or AI) must work within the project.

REPRODUCTION-CRITICAL CONTENT (ANNEX OVERFLOW):
Some files exist primarily to define large string constants, prompt templates,
configuration arrays, default value sets, or command/IDE template content.
For these files:
- Write a CONCISE summary following the standard density and length rules
- List each constant/export by name with a one-line description of its role
- Do NOT attempt to reproduce multi-line string constants verbatim in the summary
- Instead, end the summary with a dedicated ## Annex References section listing
  each reproduction-critical constant:

  ## Annex References
  - `FILE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` — system prompt for file analysis (250 lines)
  - `DIRECTORY_SYSTEM_PROMPT` — system prompt for AGENTS.md generation (150 lines)

  The pipeline will extract the actual constant values from source code and write
  them to a companion .annex.sum file automatically. Your job is to IDENTIFY which
  constants are reproduction-critical, not to reproduce them inline.

For files that are primarily logic (functions, classes, algorithms), ignore this
section — it does not apply.

OUTPUT FORMAT (MANDATORY):
- Start your response DIRECTLY with the purpose statement — a single bold line: **Purpose statement here.**
- Do NOT include any preamble, thinking, or meta-commentary before the purpose statement
- Do NOT say "Here is...", "Now I'll...", "Based on my analysis...", "Let me create...", "Perfect."
- Your response IS the documentation — not a message about the documentation
entries.response:
**Purpose:** Exports five pipeline node handler types plus shared test utilities for creating mock nodes and graphs.

## Exports

- `CodergenHandler` — code generation node handler
- `ManagerLoopHandler` — manager loop control handler
- `ParallelHandler` — parallel branch execution handler  
- `FanInHandler` — parallel branch convergence handler
- `ToolHandler` — external tool invocation handler

## Test Helpers (cfg(test))

- `make_node(id, shape, prompt, attrs) -> PipelineNode` — factory for test PipelineNode with customizable id, shape, prompt, and AttributeValue map
- `make_minimal_graph() -> PipelineGraph` — parses hardcoded `digraph G { A -> B }` DOT string into PipelineGraph

## Dependencies

- `attractor_dot::parse` — DOT string parsing for test graph creation
- `attractor_dot::AttributeValue` — attribute type for test node construction
- `PipelineGraph`, `PipelineNode` — core pipeline types from crate::graph

entries.response:
**crates/attractor-cli/src/commands/decompose.rs decomposes technical specifications into tracker tickets via Claude API.**

## Exported Functions

`cmd_decompose(spec_path: &std::path::Path, dry_run: bool) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — reads spec file, invokes Claude CLI with JSON prompt, parses epic+tasks+dependencies, creates epic via `bd create`, creates tasks via `bd create`, adds dependencies via `bd dep add`, runs `validate_decomposition`, prints summary.

`validate_decomposition(spec_content: &str, epic_id: Option<&str>) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — extracts identifiers via regex, checks field completeness on tickets (description, acceptance_criteria, design), computes spec coverage %, reports missing identifiers. Dry-run mode (epic_id=None) reports extracted identifiers without fetching tickets.

## Internal Types

`DecomposeOutput` — serde::Deserialize struct with fields: epic: EpicDef, tasks: Vec<TaskDef>, dependencies: Vec<DepDef>.

`EpicDef` — serde::Deserialize with title: String, description: String.

`TaskDef` — serde::Deserialize with title, r#type (default "task"), priority (default "P2"), description, acceptance (Option), design (Option), notes (Option).

`DepDef` — serde::Deserialize with blocked: usize, blocker: usize.

## Behavioral Contracts

Prompt template defines output JSON structure with epic, tasks, dependencies. Task fields must include description (2-4 sentences), acceptance (exact test names/assertions), design (code examples/file paths), notes (cross-references/gotchas).

Regex patterns:
- `r"(test_\w+)"` — extracts test names
- `r"(?:src|tests)/[\w/]+\.(?:rs|ndjson)"` — extracts file paths
- `r"([A-Z]\w+::\w+)"` — extracts qualified identifiers
- `r"####\s+`([^`]+)`"` — extracts code block headers

Noise filter: stdlib_prefixes (Arc, Box, HashMap, Mutex, Option, Result, String, Vec, etc.) excluded from identifier tracking. noise_suffixes (::clone, ::unwrap, ::to_string, etc.) filtered from qualified identifiers.

Code fence stripping: strips leading ```json and trailing ``` if present in Claude output.

## Integration Points

Claude CLI invocation: `claude -p <prompt> --output-format json --no-session-persistence` — expects JSON in .result field.

bd CLI: `bd create --title <title> --type <type> --priority <priority> --description <desc> --acceptance <acc> --design <des> --notes <notes> --silent` creates tickets. `bd dep add <parent> <child>` adds dependencies. `bd list --parent <epic_id> --json --limit 0` fetches child tickets.

## Error Handling

Claude CLI failure: `anyhow::bail!("Claude CLI failed: {}", stderr)`. JSON parse failure: `anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse Claude's JSON output: {}", cleaned)`. bd create failures: `anyhow::bail!("Failed to create epic/task: {}", stderr)`. Dependency add failures: eprintln warnings, non-fatal.

## Output Format (Dry Run)

`println!("Decomposition (dry run):\n")` — prints epic title, description, tasks with type/priority/description/acceptance/design/notes (truncated to 120 chars), dependencies as "Task [X] blocked by Task [Y]". Runs validation but skips ticket creation.

entries.response:
**Purpose statement here.**
**Purpose:** `ToolHandler` executes shell commands defined in pipeline nodes, capturing stdout/stderr/exit codes and returning structured outcomes with context updates.

## Exports

- `pub struct ToolHandler` — NodeHandler implementation for parallelogram-shaped tool execution nodes
- `impl NodeHandler for ToolHandler` — provides `handler_type()` returning `"tool"` and `execute()` async method

## execute() Signature

```rust
async fn execute(
    &self,
    node: &PipelineNode,
    context: &Context,
    _graph: &PipelineGraph,
) -> Result<Outcome>
```

## Required Attributes

- `tool_command: AttributeValue::String` — shell command to execute (required, errors if missing)
- `timeout: Option<Duration>` — per-node timeout override (default: 300s)

## Context Inputs

- `dry_run: bool` — if true, skips execution, returns success with `{node_id}.dry_run: true`
- `workdir: String` — working directory for spawned command

## Context Outputs

| Key | Type | Description |
|-----|------|-------------|
| `last_tool_command` | String | Command that was run |
| `{node_id}.completed` | Bool | Always `true` |
| `{node_id}.exit_code` | i64 | Exit code from command |
| `{node_id}.stdout` | String | Captured stdout |
| `{node_id}.stderr` | String | Captured stderr (if non-empty) |
| `{node_id}.dry_run` | Bool | True if dry run mode |

## Behavioral Contracts

- **Command invocation**: spawns `sh -c "<command>"` via tokio process
- **Default timeout**: 300 seconds (`std::time::Duration::from_secs(300)`)
- **Output truncation**: combined stdout+stderr truncated at 4096 bytes using valid UTF-8 boundary
- **Success判定**: `output.status.success()` → `StageStatus::Success`, else `StageStatus::Fail`
- **Failure reason format**: `"Command exited with code {exit_code}"` when status is Fail

## Error Variants

- `AttractorError::HandlerError` — missing `tool_command` or spawn/execution failure
- `AttractorError::CommandTimeout` — timeout exceeded (includes `timeout_ms` field)

## Tests

Four test cases cover: dry_run skipping, missing command error, successful execution, and failure capture with exit code propagation.

**ParallelHandler fans out to all targets via `outgoing_edges`. FanInHandler marks merge completion.**

## Exports

- `ParallelHandler` — `NodeHandler` impl; extracts `outgoing_edges` from `node.id`, returns targets as `suggested_next_ids`
- `FanInHandler` — `NodeHandler` impl; merge point returning empty `suggested_next_ids`

## Public Interface

```rust
impl NodeHandler for ParallelHandler {
    fn handler_type(&self) -> &str;  // "parallel"
    async fn execute(&self, node: &PipelineNode, _context: &Context, graph: &PipelineGraph) -> Result<Outcome>;
}

impl NodeHandler for FanInHandler {
    fn handler_type(&self) -> &str;  // "parallel.fan_in"
    async fn execute(&self, node: &PipelineNode, _context: &Context, _graph: &PipelineGraph) -> Result<Outcome>;
}
```

## Behavioral Contracts

- `ParallelHandler` builds `branch_targets: Vec<String>` via `graph.outgoing_edges(&node.id).iter().map(|e| e.to)`
- `Outcome.notes` format: `"Fan-out to {branch_count} branches"`
- Both handlers return `StageStatus::Success`, empty `context_updates: HashMap::new()`, `failure_reason: None`
- `suggested_next_ids` drives execution engine routing (not executed inline here)

## Test Patterns

- `make_node(id, shape)` helper constructs `PipelineNode` with fields: `id`, `label`, `shape`, `node_type: None`, `prompt: None`, `max_retries: 0`, `goal_gate: false`, `retry_target: None`, `fallback_retry_target: None`, `fidelity: None`, `thread_id: None`, `classes: Vec::new()`, `timeout: None`, `llm_model: None`, `llm_provider: None`, `reasoning_effort: None`, `auto_status: true`, `allow_partial: false`, `raw_attrs: HashMap::new()`
- Tests parse DOT via `attractor_dot::parse` → `PipelineGraph::from_dot`
- Tests assert `outcome.notes.contains("2 branches")`, `contains(&"branch_a".to_string())`

## Dependencies

- `attractor_types::{Context, Outcome, Result, StageStatus}` — return types and context
- `crate::graph::{PipelineGraph, PipelineNode}` — graph traversal and node representation
- `crate::handler::NodeHandler` — trait being implemented
- `attractor_dot::parse` — used in tests only

```
Nodes: {count}
Edges: {count}

```

entries.response:
**generate.rs.bak generates Graphviz DOT pipelines from spec files by invoking Claude Code CLI.**

## Exports

`cmd_generate(prd_path: Option<&Path>, spec_path: &Path, output: Option<&Path>, verbose: bool) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — Generates single pipeline from spec (required) and optional PRD, writes to output path or `pipelines/{stem}.dot`, validates via `attractor_pipeline::validate`, prints node count and validation status.

`cmd_generate_dir(dir: &Path, output_dir: Option<&Path>, verbose: bool) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<PathBuf>>` — Batch generates from directory: scans `*-spec.md` files (lexical sort), pairs each with `*-prd.md` if exists, outputs zero-padded `.dot` files to `pipelines/`.

## Internal Functions

`start_spinner(message: &str) -> SpinnerGuard` — Spawns stderr braille spinner (10 frames at 80ms intervals), returns guard that stops thread on drop.

`build_prompt(spec: &str, prd: Option<&str>) -> String` — Constructs LLM prompt with PRD section (if present), Technical Specification, Pipeline conventions (double-quoted attrs, shape semantics, edge conditions), Timeout guidance (120s-1200s scale), Structure pattern (work+verify diamonds with FAIL loops), and REQUIRED commit_changes node before done.

`extract_digraph(s: &str) -> Option<String>` — Extracts digraph from Claude output: strips markdown fences, finds `digraph` keyword, matches brace depth to find closing `}`. Handles preambles, fenced code, nested braces in prompt attributes.

`strip_code_fences(s: &str) -> String` — Removes triple-backtick fences, preserves inner content.

## Behavioral Contracts

Claude CLI invocation: `claude -p - --model sonnet --system-prompt "You are a Graphviz DOT generator. Output ONLY a raw digraph..." --settings '{"enabledPlugins":{}}' --strict-mcp-config '{}' --tools '' --output-format json --no-session-persistence`

DOT shape semantics: `"Mdiamond"` (start), `"Msquare"` (done), `"box"` (work), `"diamond"` with `node_type="conditional"` (decision), `"hexagon"` with `node_type="wait.human"` (human gate).

Timeout tiers: `"120s"` (trivial), `"300s"` (light), `"600s"` (standard), `"900s"` (heavy), `"1200s"` (intensive). Default: 600s.

Output format: ALL attribute values double-quoted. Example node:
```
node_name [
    label="Short Label"
    shape="box"
    timeout="600s"
    prompt="Detailed instructions..."
]
```

Commit step required: last work node before `done` must be `commit_changes` with `allowed_tools="Bash(git:*)"`.

## File Conventions

Input: `*-spec.md` required, `*-prd.md` optional (pair by stem replacement). Output: `pipelines/*.dot`.

## Tests

Unit tests cover `strip_code_fences` (fenced/unfenced, trailing whitespace, noop cases), `build_prompt` (PRD ordering, timeout values, commit node requirement), `extract_digraph` (raw, fenced, preambles, nested braces, braces in prompts).

entries.response:
**WaitHumanHandler pauses pipeline for human choice via edge labels.**

## Exports

- `WaitHumanHandler` — struct holding `Arc<dyn Interviewer>`; constructor `new(interviewer: Arc<dyn Interviewer>) -> Self`
- `NodeHandler` impl: `execute(node, _ctx, graph) -> Result<Outcome>`
  - `handler_type()` returns `"wait.human"` (exact string)
  - Reads outgoing edge labels as `choices`; defaults `["Continue"]` when labels empty
  - Falls back prompt from `node.prompt` to `node.label` if None
  - Passes `node.timeout` to Question; returns `StageStatus::Success` with `preferred_label` set to answer choice

## Dependencies

- `Interviewer::ask(Question) -> Result<Answer>` — trait injected via Arc
- `Question { prompt, choices, default, timeout }` — builder-like struct
- `Outcome { preferred_label: Some(answer.choice), notes: "Human responded" }`
- `PipelineGraph::outgoing_edges(node_id)` — returns edges with optional `label` field

## Behavioral Contracts

- `"wait.human"` string literal in `handler_type()` — must match node `node_type` for dispatch
- Edge labels become choice buttons; absent labels → `["Continue"]` sentinel
- `preferred_label` set to exact `answer.choice` string from interviewer
- `Outcome.notes` = `"Human responded"` (verbatim)

## Tests

- `derives_choices_from_edges` — verifies edge labels `["Approve", "Reject"]` populate choices
- `returns_preferred_label_from_answer` — verifies Answer.choice propagates to Outcome.preferred_label
- `uses_continue_when_no_edge_labels` — verifies sentinel choice when no edge labels
- `uses_label_as_prompt_fallback` — verifies `node.label` used when `node.prompt` is None
- Test helper `make_node()` constructs minimal `PipelineNode` with `node_type: Some("wait.human".to_string())`

entries.response:
**Purpose**: ManagerLoopHandler supervises sub-pipeline execution for "house" shaped nodes.

## Public Interface
- `ManagerLoopHandler` struct implements `NodeHandler` trait for `handler_type() == "stack.manager_loop"`
- `execute(&self, node: &PipelineNode, _context: &Context, _graph: &PipelineGraph) -> Result<Outcome>` — async entry point

## Behavioral Contracts
- Returns `Outcome { status: StageStatus::Success, ... }` unconditionally
- Sets context key `"${node.id}.managed"` to `serde_json::Value::Bool(true)` via `context_updates`
- Uses prompt from `node.prompt.as_deref()` or defaults to `"Supervise sub-tasks"`
- Notes format: `"Manager completed: {prompt}"`

## Tests
- `manager_handler_returns_success` — verifies `StageStatus::Success`, notes containing prompt, `outcome.context_updates["mgr.managed"] == Bool(true)`
- `manager_handler_default_prompt` — confirms fallback to `"Supervise sub-tasks"`
- `manager_handler_type` — asserts `handler_type() == "stack.manager_loop"`

entries.response:
**Purpose:** `cmd_plan` scaffolds PRD or technical specification documents from templates, optionally using Claude CLI to auto-generate content.

## Exported Symbols

`cmd_plan(prd: bool, spec: bool, from_prompt: Option<&str>, output: Option<&std::path::Path>) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — async CLI handler; validates exactly one of `--prd`/`--spec` is set, selects template via `prd-template.md`/`spec-template.md`, writes to `.pas/prd.md` or `.pas/spec.md` by default.

`copy_template(content: &str, output: &std::path::Path) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — creates parent dirs via `std::fs::create_dir_all`, writes template content.

`generate_with_claude(description: &str, template: &str, output: &std::path::Path, is_prd: bool) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — async; builds prompt with exact instructions, shells out to `claude -p <prompt> --dangerously-skip-permissions --no-session-persistence`, captures stdout, writes to output path.

## Behavioral Contracts

Templates embedded via `include_str!("../../../../templates/prd-template.md")` and `include_str!("../../../../templates/spec-template.md")`.

Prompt format: `"Generate a {} document following this exact template format:\n\n{}\n\nUser request: {}\n\nInstructions:\n1. Replace all [bracketed placeholders]...\n..."` with `doc_type` (PRD/Technical Specification), `template`, `description`, and date from `chrono::Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d")`.

Claude CLI failure: `anyhow::bail!("Claude CLI failed: {}", stderr)` where stderr is `String::from_utf8_lossy(&output_result.stderr)`.

## Integration

Uses `tokio::process::Command` for async subprocess spawning; `anyhow` for error handling; `chrono` for date formatting.

entries.response:
**launch.rs orchestrates end-to-end pipeline execution: generate .dot files from spec+PRD pairs, validate them, then run all pipelines sequentially.**

## Exported Symbols

`cmd_launch(docs_dir: &Path, output_dir: Option<&Path>, workdir: Option<&Path>, dry_run: bool, max_budget_usd: Option<f64>, max_steps: u64, fresh: bool, verbose: bool) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — orchestrates generate→validate→run workflow; returns error if no pipelines generated or validation fails.

## Integration Points

- Calls `super::cmd_generate_dir` to generate .dot files from `-spec.md` files (optionally paired with `-prd.md`)
- Calls `crate::load_pipeline` to parse generated .dot files
- Calls `attractor_pipeline::validate` to validate each pipeline
- Uses `attractor_pipeline::Severity::Error` to detect validation failures
- Calls `super::cmd_run_dir` to execute pipelines

## Behavioral Contracts

- Generated pipelines must contain files ending in `-spec.md`; zero-padded prefixes control execution order (e.g., `phase-01-spec.md`, `phase-02-spec.md`)
- `-prd.md` files are optional but recommended for pairing with specs
- Validation must pass for all pipelines before execution begins; failures abort launch with message pointing to `pipelines_dir`
- Error pattern for validation failures: `[FAIL] {filename}` followed by `[ERROR] {rule}: {message}`
- Parse errors also cause validation failure

## Error Handling

- `anyhow::bail!` used with detailed guidance when no pipelines generated
- `anyhow::bail!` used when validation fails, instructing user to fix .dot files in `pipelines_dir`
- Validation errors printed to stdout; function returns error after all pipelines processed

entries.response:
**cmd_validate validates a pipeline file and exits with code 1 on errors.**

## Interface

`cmd_validate(path: &std::path::Path) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — loads pipeline via `crate::load_pipeline`, validates via `attractor_pipeline::validate`, prints diagnostics with severity (ERROR/WARN/INFO), exits with `std::process::exit(1)` on any Error-level diagnostic.

## Behavior

Prints `[ERROR] rule: message`, `[WARN] rule: message`, or `[INFO] rule: message` per diagnostic. Prints "Pipeline is valid" when `diagnostics.is_empty()`. Uses `attractor_pipeline::Severity::Error|Warning|Info` for classification.

entries.response:
**Purpose statement here.**

**crates/attractor-cli/src/commands/generate.rs** implements the `generate` CLI command pipeline — it reads spec/PRD markdown files, calls the Claude CLI to produce a DOT digraph, validates the output, and writes pipeline files.

## Exported Symbols

- `cmd_generate(prd_path, spec_path, output, verbose) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — async, orchestrates single pipeline generation: reads files, builds prompt, spawns Claude CLI subprocess, extracts/validates DOT, writes output.
- `cmd_generate_dir(dir, output_dir, verbose) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<PathBuf>>` — async, scans dir for `*-spec.md` files, pairs each with optional `*-prd.md`, invokes `cmd_generate` per pair.
- `start_spinner(message) -> SpinnerGuard` — spawns a braille spinner thread on stderr with elapsed timer; guard stops thread on drop.
- `SpinnerGuard` — RAII guard wrapping `AtomicBool` + `JoinHandle`; `Drop` signals done flag and joins thread.
- `build_prompt(spec, prd) -> String` — assembles the LLM prompt from spec content and optional PRD; injects full DOT syntax constraints, pipeline conventions, timeout guidance, and commit step requirements.
- `extract_digraph(s) -> Option<String>` — strips markdown fences, locates `digraph` keyword, matches braces to extract the raw DOT subgraph.
- `strip_code_fences(s) -> String` — removes triple-backtick fences; returns input unchanged if not fenced.

## Prompt Template — Reproduction-Critical

`build_prompt` produces the system prompt passed to `claude -p -` with these verbatim constraints:

- **Claude invocation flags**: `--model sonnet`, `--settings '{"enabledPlugins":{}}'`, `--strict-mcp-config '{}'`, `--tools ''`, `--output-format json`, `--no-session-persistence`.
- **System prompt**: `"You are a Graphviz DOT generator. Output ONLY a raw digraph. No commentary, no markdown fences, no skill invocations, no function calls."`
- **DOT subset constraints** (CRITICAL — verbatim in prompt):
  - `digraph Name { }` only, no `graph`/`strict`/`--`
  - Node IDs: `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*` (bare identifiers only)
  - NO quoted node IDs, numeric IDs, HTML labels, or port syntax
  - All attribute values double-quoted: `shape="Mdiamond"` not `shape=Mdiamond`
  - NO chained `[a=1][b=2]` blocks
  - String concatenation not supported
  - Attribute separators: commas, semicolons, or whitespace inside `[ ]`
  - Comments: `//` line and `/* */` block only
- **Pipeline conventions**: shapes `Mdiamond` (start), `Msquare` (done), `box` (work), `diamond` (node_type="conditional"), `hexagon` (node_type="wait.human"), `parallelogram` (tool_command shell nodes); graph attrs `label`, `goal`, `model="sonnet"`; node attrs `prompt`, `allowed_tools`, `goal_gate`, `llm_model`, `tool_command`; edge attrs `label`, `condition` (preferred_label=PASS), `loop_restart="true"`.
- **Human gate rule**: hexagon nodes only for spec-explicit human approval; automatable checks MUST use parallelogram/box tool nodes with curl/wget — never gate HTTP verifications behind human gates.
- **Timeout tiers** (verbatim): `timeout="120s"` (trivial), `timeout="300s"` (light), `timeout="600s"` (standard, default), `timeout="900s"` (heavy), `timeout="1200s"` (intensive); "When in doubt, use 600s".
- **Commit step** (REQUIRED, verbatim): `commit_changes` node with `shape="box"`, `timeout="120s"`, `allowed_tools="Bash(git:*)"`, prompt runs `git diff --stat` → `git add -A` → commit with descriptive message.

## Pipeline File Naming & Pairing Conventions

- Single spec: output defaults to `pipelines/{stem}.dot` or `--output` path.
- Directory batch: glob `*-spec.md` files, sort lexically, pair with `*-prd.md` (optional), output to `pipelines/{stem}.dot`; zero-padded prefixes control order (e.g. `phase-01-spec.md`).
- Parent directories auto-created via `create_dir_all`.

## Validation Flow

1. Claude stdout parsed as JSON; requires `result` field.
2. `extract_digraph` called on `result` string (handles preamble, fences, nested braces).
3. `crate::load_pipeline` parses as DOT; on failure, prints first 500 chars of DOT and exits.
4. `attractor_pipeline::validate` runs diagnostics; errors printed with `[ERROR]` prefix and rule name.
5. Exit status: 0 on success (pipeline generated, validation status reported); `anyhow::bail!` on parse failures or missing digraph.

## Dependencies

- `tokio::process::Command` for async subprocess spawning; `wait_with_output` for capture.
- `tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt::write_all` for async stdin write.
- `anyhow::anyhow!` / `anyhow::bail!` for error context with `display()` paths.
- `serde_json` for response parsing; extracts `result`, `total_cost_usd`, `usage` in verbose mode.
- `attractor_pipeline::validate`, `crate::load_pipeline` for post-generation validation.

## Test Coverage (`#[cfg(test)]`)

Unit tests cover: `strip_code_fences` (6 cases: dot/graphviz/plain fences, trailing whitespace, no-op, preserves inner), `build_prompt` (8 cases: spec-only, with PRD, pipeline conventions, human gate discouragement, DOT syntax constraints, timeout guidance, commit step requirement, raw digraph request, PRD-before-spec ordering), `extract_digraph` (6 cases: raw, fenced, preamble, nested braces, fenced-with-preamble, no-digraph, braces-in-prompts).

entries.response:
**Purpose statement: run.rs exports cmd_run and cmd_run_dir which execute .dot pipeline files either individually or in batch with cross-file resume capability.**

## Exported Functions

`fn stable_logs_dir(pipeline_path: &Path) -> PathBuf` — derives deterministic logs dir from pipeline path using canonical path hash; format is `.pas/logs/<stem>-<8hex>` for checkpoint persistence.

`pub async fn cmd_run(path: &Path, workdir: Option<&Path>, logs: Option<&Path>, dry_run: bool, max_budget_usd: Option<f64>, max_steps: u64, fresh: bool) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — executes single pipeline; supports workdir override, explicit logs dir, dry-run mode, budget/steps limits, and fresh flag to clear checkpoint. Sets context keys: `workdir`, `dry_run`, `max_budget_usd`, `max_steps`. Prints goal, logs path, resume status, and cost summary.

`pub async fn cmd_run_dir(dir: &Path, workdir: Option<&Path>, dry_run: bool, max_budget_usd: Option<f64>, max_steps: u64, fresh: bool) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — runs all `*.dot` files in directory lexically sorted; uses zero-padded names for ordering control; maintains `manifest.json` at `.pas/logs/<stem>-batch-<8hex>` for cross-file resume. Skips completed pipelines, cleans manifest on success.

## Internal Types & Functions

`struct RunManifest { completed: Vec<String>, current: Option<String> }` — JSON-serializable manifest tracking completed pipelines and current executing pipeline.

`fn stable_manifest_dir(dir: &Path) -> PathBuf` — generates manifest storage path using same hashing scheme as `stable_logs_dir`, format `.pas/logs/<stem>-batch-<8hex>`.

`fn load_manifest(path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<RunManifest>` — loads existing manifest or returns default if absent.

`fn save_manifest(manifest: &RunManifest, path: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — writes manifest with parent directory creation.

## Key Behaviors

Checkpoint file is `checkpoint.json` in logs dir; checked via `logs_dir.join("checkpoint.json").exists()`.

Total cost extracted from context keys matching `*.cost_usd` pattern.

Exit cleans up manifest only on full completion — partial runs retain manifest for resume.

entries.response:
**crates/attractor-cli/src/commands/scaffold.rs scaffolds a pipeline DOT file from a bd epic ID.**

## Exported Symbols

`cmd_scaffold(epic_id: &str, output: Option<&std::path::Path>) -> anyhow::Result<()>` — async command that fetches epic details via `bd show --json`, replaces placeholders in embedded `epic-runner.dot` template, writes pipeline to filesystem, and validates it.

## Integration Points

- Template: `include_str!("../../../../templates/epic-runner.dot")` embeds epic-runner.dot
- CLI invocation: `tokio::process::Command::new("bd").arg("show").arg(epic_id).arg("--json")`
- Pipeline loader: `crate::load_pipeline(&output_path)`
- Validator: `attractor_pipeline::validate(&graph)` returns diagnostics with `Severity::Error`

## Behavioral Contracts

- Goal replacement pattern (verbatim): `goal="Implement all child tasks of epic EPIC_ID, closing each as completed."`
- Output path default format: `pipelines/{epic_id}.dot`
- JSON response expectation: bd returns array with one element containing `title` and `description` fields
- Validation triggers warning but NOT failure — scaffold succeeds even with errors

## Error Handling

- Checks `output_result.status.success()` and bails on stderr if bd fails
- JSON parse uses `epic_array.as_array().and_then(|arr| arr.first())` with `ok_or_else` for empty array
- Validation errors printed to stdout, do not halt execution

entries.response:
**GrepTool implements the Tool trait for regex-based file content search.**

## Exports
- `GrepTool` struct
- `MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS: usize = 20_000` — output truncation threshold

## Tool Implementation

`definition(&self) -> ToolDefinition` returns name="grep", description, JSON schema with `pattern` (required string), `path` (optional string), `case_insensitive` (optional bool).

`execute(&self, Value, &dyn ExecutionEnvironment) -> Result<String>` extracts pattern (errors with `AttractorError::ToolError { tool: "grep" }` if missing), resolves path from args or `env.working_directory()`, builds `GrepOptions { case_insensitive, include_line_numbers: true }`, calls `env.grep(pattern, path, &options)`, truncates via `truncate_output(&output, MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS, TruncationMode::Tail)`.

## Integration
- Depends on `ExecutionEnvironment::grep()` and `GrepOptions` from crate::environment
- Uses `truncate_output()` + `TruncationMode::Tail` from crate::truncation
- Returns type: `attractor_types::Result<String>`

entries.response:
**ReadFileTool reads filesystem files with line-numbered output, truncating at 50,000 chars.**

## Exports

`MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS: usize = 50_000` — truncation threshold for output.

`ReadFileTool` struct implements `Tool` trait with `definition()` and `execute()`.

## ToolDefinition Schema

- `name`: `"read_file"`
- `description`: `"Read a file from the filesystem. Returns content with line numbers."`
- `parameters` (JSON schema):
  - `file_path` (string, **required**) — absolute path
  - `offset` (integer, optional) — 1-based line start
  - `limit` (integer, optional, **default: 2000**) — max lines

## execute() Behavior

1. Extracts `file_path` (required), `offset` (1-based → 0-indexed conversion), `limit` (default 2000)
2. Calls `env.read_file(Path::new(file_path))` via `ExecutionEnvironment`
3. Splits content into lines, applies offset/limit slicing with `saturating_sub(1)` for offset
4. Formats output: `{:>width$} | {}` pattern for line-numbered output, width computed from end line count
5. Returns `truncate_output(&output, MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS, TruncationMode::HeadTail)`

## Error Handling

Missing `file_path` returns `AttractorError::ToolError { tool: "read_file", message: "file_path is required" }`.

## Dependencies

`async_trait`, `serde_json::json!`, `std::path::Path`; couples with `ExecutionEnvironment`, `ToolDefinition`, `truncate_output`, `TruncationMode::HeadTail`.

entries.response:
**Purpose:** EditFileTool executes in-place string replacement on files via the Tool trait, requiring exact old_string matches and supporting single or global replacement modes.

## Exports
- `EditFileTool` — struct implementing Tool trait for file editing
- `definition()` → `ToolDefinition` with `name: "edit_file"`, `description`, JSON schema params (`file_path`, `old_string`, `new_string`, `replace_all: bool`)
- `execute(arguments, env)` → `Result<String>` — reads file via `env.read_file`, validates old_string non-empty, counts matches via `.matches()`, errors on 0 matches or multiple matches without `replace_all`, calculates line numbers for multi-match error, writes via `env.write_file`

## Parameters
| Param | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| `file_path` | string | yes | Absolute path |
| `old_string` | string | yes | Exact match, non-empty |
| `new_string` | string | yes | Replacement |
| `replace_all` | boolean | no | Default false |

## Validation Rules
- `old_string` must be non-empty → `AttractorError::ToolError` with `"old_string must not be empty"`
- `old_string` must exist in file → `AttractorError::ToolError` with `"old_string not found in {file_path}"`
- Multiple matches without `replace_all` → error with line numbers format: `format!("old_string found {} times in {} (at lines: {:?})..."`

## Dependencies
- `ExecutionEnvironment` trait for `env.read_file(path)` and `env.write_file(path, content)`
- `attractor_types::Result<String>` and `AttractorError::ToolError` variant
- Uses `.matches(old_string).count()` for match counting, `.replacen(old_string, new_string, 1)` for single replacement, `.replace(old_string, new_string)` for global

## Error Message Patterns
`"old_string not found in {file_path}"` — requires exact file_path variable interpolation
`"old_string found {count} times in {file_path} (at lines: {Vec<u32>}). Use replace_all=true to replace all occurrences."` — multi-occurrence error includes line numbers array

entries.response:
**Purpose statement:**

**`codergen_handler.rs` shells out to Claude Code, Codex CLI, or Gemini CLI for pipeline `codergen` nodes, parses their JSON/JSONL output, and emits context updates with the response text and optional cost/turns metrics.**

## Exports

| Symbol | Type | Signature |
|--------|------|-----------|
| `CodergenHandler` | struct | `pub struct CodergenHandler` — implements `NodeHandler` |
| `LlmCliProvider` | enum | `Claude \| Codex \| Gemini` with `FromStr`, `binary_name()`, `display_name()`, `from_node()` |
| `CliRunConfig` | struct | `CliRunConfig<'a>` — holds `provider`, `prompt`, `model`, `workdir`, `node`, `graph` |
| `build_cli_command` | fn | `fn build_cli_command(cfg: &CliRunConfig<'_>) -> tokio::process::Command` |
| `parse_cli_output` | fn | `fn parse_cli_output(provider, stdout, stderr, node_id) -> Result<NormalizedCliResult>` |
| `parse_claude_output` | fn | `fn parse_claude_output(stdout, node_id) -> Result<NormalizedCliResult>` |
| `parse_codex_output` | fn | `fn parse_codex_output(stdout, node_id) -> Result<NormalizedCliResult>` |
| `parse_gemini_output` | fn | `fn parse_gemini_output(stdout, node_id) -> Result<NormalizedCliResult>` |
| `extract_label` | fn | `fn extract_label(response: &str, labels: &[String]) -> Option<String>` |

## Parsing Structs

| Struct | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `ClaudeOutput` | Deserializes `{"result", "is_error", "subtype", "total_cost_usd", "num_turns"}` from `claude -p --output-format json` |
| `CodexEvent` | Tagged enum for JSONL events: `item.completed`, `turn.completed`, `turn.failed`, `error`, `Other` |
| `CodexItem` | `{"type", "text?"}` inside `ItemCompleted` |
| `CodexUsage` | `{"input_tokens", "output_tokens", "cached_input_tokens?"}` inside `TurnCompleted` |
| `CodexError` | `{"message"}` inside `TurnFailed` |
| `GeminiOutput` | Deserializes `{"session_id?", "response?", "error?"}` from Gemini JSON |
| `GeminiError` | `{"type", "message", "code?"}` inside `GeminiOutput` |
| `NormalizedCliResult` | Normalized result: `text`, `is_error`, `cost_usd?`, `turns?`, `raw_output` |

## CLI Argument Conventions (Behavioral Contracts)

### Claude
- `-p` + `--output-format json` + `--no-session-persistence` + `--dangerously-skip-permissions` + `--strict-mcp-config` + `--disable-slash-commands`
- `--model` if `llm_model` set
- `--allowedTools` from `node.raw_attrs["allowed_tools"]` (Claude only)
- `--max-budget-usd` from `node.raw_attrs["max_budget_usd"]` (Claude only)

### Codex
- `--json` + `--yolo` + `--skip-git-repo-check` + `--ephemeral`
- Prompt is **POSITIONAL** (last arg) — NOT `-p` (that's `--profile` in Codex)
- `--cd` for workdir

### Gemini
- `--output-format json` + `--approval-mode yolo`
- Prompt is **POSITIONAL** — `-p`/`--prompt` deprecated
- **No `--cwd` flag** — working dir set via `cmd.current_dir()` only

## `CodegenHandler::execute` Behavior

1. Reads `node.prompt` (required), `node.label`, `node.llm_provider` (default: `claude`), `node.llm_model`, `node.timeout` (default: 600s)
2. Resolves `llm_model`: node attribute → graph-level `attrs["model"]` fallback
3. Resolves `workdir` from `context["workdir"]`
4. Builds full prompt: `goal` header → prior `.result`/`.output` context keys → task label + prompt
5. For `diamond` or `conditional` nodes: appends `"You MUST end your response with exactly one of these labels on its own line: {labels}"` to prompt
6. Spawns CLI process; returns `AttractorError::CliNotFound` if binary missing
7. **Timeout**: captures `child.id()` BEFORE `wait_with_output()`, then on timeout sends `SIGKILL` to PID (Unix only) to kill process tree
8. Parses stdout via provider-specific parser; returns error if stdout empty (includes first 500 chars of stderr)
9. For conditional nodes: calls `extract_label(cli_result.text, edge_labels)` to find preferred routing label
10. Context updates: `{node_id}.completed`, `{node_id}.result`, `{node_id}.provider`, `{node_id}.cost_usd?`, `{node_id}.turns?`, `{node_id}.label?`

## `extract_label` Algorithm

1. Reverses response lines, takes first 5, checks for exact case-insensitive match against labels
2. Fallback: uppercase scan of full response for label substring
3. Returns first match or `None`

## `LlmCliProvider::from_str` Mapping

| Input | Resolves to |
|-------|-------------|
| `"claude"` / `"anthropic"` | `Claude` |
| `"codex"` / `"openai"` | `Codex` |
| `"gemini"` / `"google"` | `Gemini` |
| unknown | `Claude` (logs warning) |

## Node Attributes (Configuration)

| Attribute | Provider | Description |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `prompt` | all | Task prompt (required) |
| `llm_provider` | all | `"claude"` \| `"codex"` \| `"gemini"` (default: `"claude"`) |
| `llm_model` | all | Model override (e.g., `"sonnet"`, `"o3"`, `"gemini-2.5-pro"`) |
| `allowed_tools` | Claude | Comma-separated tool list via `--allowedTools` |
| `max_budget_usd` | Claude | Spending cap via `--max-budget-usd` |
| `timeout` | all | Duration before SIGKILL (default: 600s) |

## Context Keys (Output)

| Key | Type | Set when |
|-----|------|----------|
| `{id}.completed` | `bool` | always |
| `{id}.result` | `string` | always |
| `{id}.provider` | `string` | always (e.g., `"Claude Code"`, `"Codex CLI"`, `"Gemini CLI"`) |
| `{id}.cost_usd` | `f64` | Claude with cost data |
| `{id}.turns` | `u32` | Claude with turn count |
| `{id}.label` | `string` | diamond/conditional nodes after `extract_label` |
| `{id}.dry_run` | `bool` | dry run mode |

## Dry Run Mode

Set via `context["dry_run"] = true`; skips CLI invocation, returns `StageStatus::Success` with `"Dry run — prompt not sent: {prompt}"` in result.

entries.response:
**GlobTool finds files matching glob patterns and returns newline-separated paths.**

## Exports

- `GlobTool` — struct implementing `Tool` trait
- `MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS: usize = 20_000` — truncation threshold

## Public Interface

```rust
impl Tool for GlobTool {
    fn definition(&self) -> ToolDefinition;
    async fn execute(
        &self,
        arguments: serde_json::Value,
        env: &dyn ExecutionEnvironment,
    ) -> attractor_types::Result<String>;
}
```

Parameters: `pattern: string` (required), `path: string` (optional, defaults to working directory).

## Key Details

Implements the `Tool` trait from `async_trait`. Requires `pattern` argument — returns error `"pattern is required"` if missing. Calls `env.glob_files(pattern, path)` for file matching. Output is newline-joined paths, truncated to 20k chars via `truncate_output(&output, MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS, TruncationMode::Tail)`.

## Dependencies

- `ExecutionEnvironment` — provides `working_directory()` and `glob_files(pattern, path)` method
- `ToolDefinition` — JSON schema via `serde_json::json!` macro with `pattern` and `path` properties
- `truncate_output` — from `crate::truncation` module

## Error Handling

Pattern validation via `.ok_or_else()` with `AttractorError::ToolError { tool: "glob", message: "pattern is required" }`.

entries.response:
**xterm-setup.js initializes xterm.js terminal with WebSocket backend.**

## Exports

- `initTerminal(containerId, folderPath)` — creates xterm instance, connects to `/api/terminal/ws` WebSocket, registers in `window._terminalInstances`
- `disposeTerminal(containerId)` — closes WebSocket, disposes terminal, clears ResizeObserver, removes sessionStorage key, deletes from registry

## Constants

- `RECONNECT_DELAY = 1000` — reconnection interval in ms

## Dependencies

- `Terminal` from `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@xterm/xterm@5.5.0/+esm`
- `FitAddon` from `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@xterm/addon-fit@0.10.0/+esm`
- `WebLinksAddon` from `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@xterm/addon-web-links@0.11.0/+esm`

## Theme

Catppuccin Mocha palette: background `#1e1e2e`, foreground `#cdd6f4`, cursor `#f5e0dc`, 16 color variants.

## WebSocket Protocol

- URL: `wss://` or `ws://` + `location.host` + `/api/terminal/ws?session=<id>&folder=<path>`
- Session ID stored in `sessionStorage` with key `terminal_session_` + containerId
- Binary: `{ type: 'resize', cols, rows }` sent as JSON; ArrayBuffer received as `Uint8Array` written to terminal
- Session message: `{ type: 'session', session_id }` triggers `sessionStorage.setItem`
- Reconnect display: `\r\n\x1b[33m[Reconnecting...]\x1b[0m\r\n`

## State Management

- `window._terminalInstances[containerId]` stores `{ ws, terminal, reconnectTimer, fitAddon, resizeObserver }`
- `ResizeObserver` calls `fitAddon.fit()` on container resize

## Cleanup Contract

`disposeTerminal` must: close WebSocket, dispose terminal, clear reconnect timeout, disconnect ResizeObserver, remove sessionStorage key, delete registry entry.

entries.response:
**WriteFileTool writes file content to disk via the tool interface.**

## Exports

`WriteFileTool` struct implementing `Tool` trait with `definition()` returning `ToolDefinition` and `execute(arguments: Value, env: &dyn ExecutionEnvironment) -> Result<String>`.

## Parameters

`file_path: string` (absolute path required), `content: string` (required).

## Behavior

Calls `env.write_file(Path::new(file_path), content).await`. Returns `"Successfully wrote {} bytes to {}"` format string. Fails with `AttractorError::ToolError` if either `file_path` or `content` missing from arguments.

## Dependencies

`async_trait::async_trait`, `serde_json::json`, `crate::environment::ExecutionEnvironment`, `crate::tool::{Tool, ToolDefinition}`, `attractor_types::{Result, AttractorError}`.

entries.response:
**ApprovalBar renders an "Approve & Execute" button that triggers the decompose→scaffold→pipeline execution chain.**

## Exported Symbols

`ApprovalBar<F>(project_id: i64, enabled: impl Fn() -> bool + Send + Sync + Copy + 'static, on_approve: F) -> impl IntoView` — Leptos component displaying button or progress spinner based on execution phase.

## Dependencies

`start_execution(project_id).await` from `crate::server::execute` initiates execution. `ExecutionPhase` enum (Decomposing, Scaffolding, Running) drives progress UI state. `Action::new` handles async execution with built-in pending state tracking.

## State Management

`signal(Option::<ExecutionPhase>::None)` stores current phase. `Effect::new` watches `execute_action.value()` for completion, calling `on_approve(response.session_id)` on success or setting phase to None on failure.

## Rendering Logic

When `phase.get()` is Some, renders `.approval-progress` div with spinner and phase-specific label. When None, renders `.btn btn-approve` button disabled when `!enabled()` or `is_loading()`. Click handler dispatches action and sets phase to `ExecutionPhase::Decomposing`.

## Integration Points

`on_approve: F` callback receives session_id string from successful execution. Parent controls enable state via `enabled()` closure. `project_id` passed to backend execution handler.

entries.response:
**ShellTool executes shell commands with configurable timeout and truncates output to 30k chars.**

## Exports

- `ShellTool` — struct implementing `Tool` trait
- `MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS: usize = 30_000` — truncation threshold

## Interface

- `definition(&self) -> ToolDefinition` — returns tool schema with `name: "shell"`, `description: "Execute a shell command."`
- `execute(&self, arguments: serde_json::Value, env: &dyn ExecutionEnvironment) -> attractor_types::Result<String>` — parses `command: string` (required), `timeout_ms: integer` (default 10000), calls `env.exec_command()`, formats output as `"Exit code: {}\nStdout:\n{}\nStderr:\n{}"` and applies `truncate_output` with `TruncationMode::HeadTail`

## Error Handling

Missing `command` returns `attractor_types::AttractorError::ToolError { tool: "shell", message: "command is required" }`

## Dependencies

- `async_trait` for async trait implementation
- `serde_json` for parameter parsing
- `ExecutionEnvironment` trait for command execution
- `ToolDefinition`, `truncate_output`, `TruncationMode` from crate modules

entries.response:
**document_viewer.rs** renders live PRD/Spec documents via SSE with SQLite caching fallback.

## Exports

`DocumentViewer<FP, FS>` — Leptos `#[component]` taking `project_id: i64`, `on_prd_change: FP`, `on_spec_change: FS` callbacks; renders dual-view (tabbed at narrow widths, split columns at wide); hydrates only.

`DocTab` — enum `{ Prd, Spec }` for tab state.

`DocumentUpdate` — `#[cfg(feature = "hydrate")]` struct for SSE deserialization with `doc_type: String`, `content: Option<String>`.

## Key Behavior

`Resource::new` fetches `get_cached_documents(project_id)` on mount for instant display.

SSE connects to `/api/documents/stream?project_id=<id>` with `gloo_net::eventsource::futures::EventSource`, subscribes to `"document_update"` channel, updates `prd_content`/`spec_content` signals on `"prd"`/`"spec"` doc_type matches.

`render_markdown` (from `crate::components::markdown_render`) converts signal strings to HTML.

Placeholder prompts: `"Create a PRD for ..."` and `"Now create the technical spec"`.

CSS class contract: `.doc-tabbed` (narrow), `.doc-column-prd`/`.doc-column-spec` (wide), `.markdown-rendered`, `.doc-placeholder`, `.doc-tab active`.

## Constraints

`FP`/`FS` bounds: `Fn(bool) + Copy + Send + Sync + 'static`.

SSE disabled when `#[cfg(feature = "hydrate")]` is unset.

entries.response:
**ExecutionNode renders expandable pipeline node with status, label, cost.**

## Exported Symbols

`NodeStatus` enum: Pending, InProgress, Success, Failed, Skipped

`ExecutionNode(_node_id: String, label: String, status: NodeStatus, content: String, cost: f64) -> impl IntoView`

## Behavior

Status maps to CSS classes: `"pending"`, `"in-progress"`, `"success"`, `"failed"`, `"skipped"`

Status maps to icons: ○ (Pending/Skipped), ● (InProgress), ✓ (Success), ✗ (Failed)

Click header to expand; content renders in `<pre>` only when expanded AND non-empty

Cost formatted as `"${:.2}"` (currency display)

## Dependencies

Leptos `prelude::*` for component macro, signals, view! macro

Serde `Serialize`, `Deserialize` for JSON interop

entries.response:
**folder_picker.rs provides a Leptos modal component for selecting project folders via text input or directory browser.**

## Exports

`FolderPicker<F>(on_select: F, on_close: impl Fn() + Clone + 'static) -> impl IntoView` — renders modal with two selection methods; `F` bound to `Fn(Project) + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static`, `on_close` closes modal on Escape key or overlay click.

## Dependencies

- `crate::server::projects::{list_directory, open_project, DirEntry, Project}` — backend RPC for directory listing and project opening
- `leptos::{prelude::*, task::spawn_local, web_sys::*}` — reactive state, async tasks, DOM event types

## State Signals

`path_input`, `input_error`, `input_loading` — text input path, error message, loading flag. `current_path`, `dir_entries`, `browser_loading`, `browser_error` — browser navigation state (current directory, entries, loading, error). Signals paired with setters via `signal()`.

## Handler Logic

- **Effect (mount)**: Calls `list_directory("")` then falls back to `$HOME` or `/Users` for initial path
- **`handle_input_open`**: Validates non-empty path, calls `open_project(path)`, invokes `on_select(project)` and `on_close()` on success
- **`handle_nav`**: Calls `list_directory(path)` to navigate into directory
- **`handle_browser_select`**: Calls `open_project(current_path.get())` to open selected folder
- **`handle_keydown`**: Closes modal when `ev.key() == "Escape"`

All handlers use `spawn_local` for async backend calls, setting loading flags before/after RPC.

## UI Structure

Modal overlay with `stop_propagation()` on inner div prevents close-on-click. Input section: text field with placeholder `/Users/you/projects/my-app`, Open button shows "Opening..." when loading. Divider labeled "OR". Browser section: breadcrumb showing `current_path`, directory list using `<For>` over `dir_entries` with folder emoji (`\u{1F4C1}`), Select This Folder button.

## Error Handling

Text input errors displayed via `error-message` class. Browser errors displayed similarly when `!browser_error.get().is_empty()`. All errors are strings from `Result` `.to_string()`.

## Key Patterns

- Leptos `<Component>` macro with `#[component]` attribute
- `move |_|` closures for Effect dependency tracking
- `.into_any()` for branching view branches
- `event_target_value(&ev)` for input value extraction

entries.response:
**Purpose:** ExecutionPanel component subscribes to SSE at `/api/stream/{session_id}`, processes live pipeline events, and renders node execution progress.

## Exports

- `ExecutionPanel<F>` — Leptos component accepting `session_id: F` where `F: Fn() -> String + Send + Sync + Copy + 'static`; returns `impl IntoView`
- `PipelineEvent` — `#[cfg(feature = "hydrate")] struct` with serde field renames: `event_type`, `node_id`, `label`, `status`, `cost_usd`, `notes`, `message`
- `NodeData` — internal struct holding `node_id`, `label`, `status: NodeStatus`, `content`, `cost: f64`
- `process_event` — `#[cfg(feature = "hydrate")] fn` handling SSE event dispatch to signals

## Signal State

`nodes: WriteSignal<Vec<NodeData>>`, `total_cost: WriteSignal<f64>`, `is_running: WriteSignal<bool>`, `error: WriteSignal<Option<String>>`

## Event Handling

`process_event` routes on `event_type`:
- `"node_start"` — inserts `NodeData` with `NodeStatus::InProgress`
- `"node_complete"` — maps `status` string to `NodeStatus::{Success,Failed,Skipped}`; sets `cost` and `content`
- `"pipeline_complete"` — sets `is_running = false`
- `"error"` — sets error message, sets `is_running = false`

## Dependencies & Conditional Compilation

Requires `#[cfg(feature = "hydrate")]`: `gloo_net::eventsource::futures::EventSource`, `futures::StreamExt`, `serde::Deserialize`. Depends on `crate::components::execution_node::{ExecutionNode, NodeStatus}`. Falls back to no-op when `hydrate` disabled.

## Output Structure

Renders `ExecutionNode` components via `<For each=...>` keyed on `node_id`. Header shows `"Running"`/`"Done"` badge and `format!("${:.2}", total_cost)`. Error state renders `<div class="execution-error">`.

## Annex References

- `NODE_STATUS_MAPPING` — `process_event` status string mapping (`"Success"`, `"Failed"`, `"Fail"`, `"Skipped"`) to `NodeStatus` enum variants

entries.response:
**ProjectSidebar renders project list sidebar with add/select/close functionality.**

## Exports
- `ProjectSidebar(projects: RwSignal<Vec<Project>>, active_project_id: RwSignal<Option<i64>>) -> impl IntoView` — Leptos component accepting projects list and active project ID as reactive signals

## Dependencies
- `FolderPicker` from `crate::components::folder_picker` — modal dialog for folder selection
- `close_project` from `crate::server::projects` — async backend function for project closure
- `RwSignal`, `signal`, `spawn_local` from leptos — reactive state and async task primitives

## Handlers
- `handle_project_selected` — adds project to list if absent, sets active ID, closes picker
- `handle_close_project` — calls `close_project(project_id).await`, removes from local list, updates active if needed
- `handle_select_project` — switches active project ID to clicked project

## State
- `show_picker: Signal<bool>` — local signal controlling FolderPicker modal visibility

## UI Structure
- Header with "+ New Project" button triggering picker
- Project list via `For` component keyed by `project.id` with active/inactive styling
- Conditional FolderPicker rendering when `show_picker.get()` is true

## Error Handling
- `close_project` result discarded with `let _ =` — failures silently ignored

entries.response:
**Terminal renders an xterm.js terminal container and initializes it via `window.initTerminal()`.**

## Exported Symbols

`Terminal(#[prop(into)] folder: String, #[prop(into)] container_id: String) -> impl IntoView` — Leptos component that renders `<div id=container_id class="terminal-container">` and hydrates xterm.js on client. Props: `folder` (absolute path to project directory for PTY spawn), `container_id` (unique terminal instance ID).

## JS Interop

- Calls `window.initTerminal(container_id, folder)` via `js_sys::Reflect` after 50ms `setTimeout` to ensure Leptos hydration completes
- Calls `window.disposeTerminal(container_id)` on component unmount via `on_cleanup`
- Only executes interop in `hydrate` feature (client-side)

## Dependencies

`leptos::prelude::*` for component macros and reactive primitives; `wasm_bindgen::prelude::*`, `web_sys`, `js_sys` for JS interop.

## Behavioral Contracts

- JS global function: `initTerminal(id: string, folder: string) -> void`
- JS global function: `disposeTerminal(id: string) -> void`
- Hydration delay: 50ms (not rAF) to guarantee DOM flush
- PTY WebSocket endpoint: `/api/terminal/ws` (referenced in docstring)

entries.response:
**ProjectView renders a two-column project layout with Terminal + Document/Execution panels.**

## Exports

`RightPanel` — enum with `Documents` and `Execution` variants controlling right panel mode.

`ProjectView` — Leptos component accepting `project: Project` prop, returning `impl IntoView`. Uses signals for panel state (`RightPanel`), session ID (`Option<String>`), and document existence flags (`prd_exists`, `spec_exists`). Renders header with project name, conditional `ApprovalBar` (enabled when both PRD and spec exist), and split view with `Terminal` (left) + `DocumentViewer`/`ExecutionPanel` (right).

## Dependencies

`Project` from `crate::server::projects` provides `id`, `folder_path`, and `name` fields. Child components: `ApprovalBar`, `DocumentViewer`, `ExecutionPanel`, `Terminal`. Uses Leptos `signal`, `view!`, `IntoView` for reactive state and rendering.

## State Management

Three signals manage UI state: `panel` (current `RightPanel`), `session_id` (approval flow), and existence flags `prd_exists`/`spec_exists` derived from `DocumentViewer` callbacks. Computed `can_approve` closure gates approval button when both documents exist.

## Layout Structure

Container classes: `app-layout` → `app-header` (title + actions) → `app-panels` (flex row) → `panel-left` (Terminal) + `panel-right` (conditional viewer). Panel mode toggles CSS class `documents-mode`.

entries.response:
**crates/attractor-web/src/server/documents.rs watches `.pas/` directory for PRD/Spec changes and streams updates via SSE.**

## Exports

`DocumentUpdate` — struct with `doc_type: String` and `content: Option<String>` fields representing prd/spec document changes.

`DocumentWatcher` — struct wrapping `broadcast::Sender<DocumentUpdate>` and `notify::RecommendedWatcher`; implements file watching for `.pas/` directory.

`DocumentWatcher::new(PathBuf, sqlx::SqlitePool, i64) -> Result<Self, notify::Error>` — initializes filesystem watcher on `.pas/` directory, filters for `prd.md` and `spec.md` files only, broadcasts updates via channel, spawns async task to persist content to SQLite via `crate::server::db::upsert_document`.

`DocumentWatcher::subscribe() -> broadcast::Receiver<DocumentUpdate>` — returns receiver for live update stream.

`DocStreamParams` — deserializable query params with `project_id: i64` for SSE endpoint.

`document_stream(Query<DocStreamParams>, State<AppState>) -> Sse<BoxStream<'static, Result<Event, Infallible>>>` — SSE handler at `GET /api/documents/stream?project_id=<id>`; loads initial documents via `crate::server::db::get_documents`, creates/gets watcher via `get_or_create_watcher`, chains initial events with live `BroadcastStream` updates, serializes as `{"doc_type": "...", "content": "..."}` with event name `document_update`.

`get_or_create_watcher(&AppState, i64, &str) -> Result<Arc<DocumentWatcher>, String>` — lazy initialization pattern; caches watchers in `state.watchers` map keyed by `project_id`, creates `PathBuf` from folder_path + ".pas".

## Patterns

Watchguard pattern: only processes `notify::EventKind::Modify` and `Create` events for `.pas/` directory, ignores other file operations.

Lazy watcher registry: `get_or_create_watcher` holds mutex on `state.watchers`, creates on first access, caches subsequent requests.

Dual delivery: pushes updates via SSE for live UI sync AND writes to SQLite via spawned async task for persistence.

Error fallback: returns SSE error event with `"error"` event name when project lookup or watcher creation fails; returns empty initial events on DB load failure.

## Integration Points

`notify::RecommendedWatcher` — filesystem events from `notify` crate for `.pas/` directory monitoring.

`tokio::sync::broadcast` — channel for pub/sub between file watcher and SSE endpoint.

`tokio_stream::wrappers::BroadcastStream` — converts broadcast receiver to Stream for SSE chaining.

`crate::server::db::upsert_document` — async persistence of document content to SQLite.

`crate::server::db::get_documents` — loads initial prd/spec content on SSE connection.

`crate::server::db::get_project` — looks up project folder_path from database.

`AppState` — shared application state containing `db: SqlitePool` and `watchers: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<i64, Arc<DocumentWatcher>>>>`.

entries.response:
**Purpose statement: `db.rs` manages SQLite-backed project and document persistence for the web server via `sqlx`.**

## Exported Types

`Project { id: i64, folder_path: String, name: String }` — represents a tracked project folder; `serde` serializable.  
`CachedDoc { doc_type: String, content: String }` — represents cached document content keyed by `doc_type` string.

## Public Functions

`init_db() -> Result<SqlitePool, sqlx::Error>` — initializes SQLite pool at `~/.pas/web.db` (auto-creates dir); runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` for `projects` and `documents` schemas; `max_connections = 5`.  
`upsert_project(pool: &SqlitePool, folder_path: &str) -> Result<Project, sqlx::Error>` — creates project if absent (name = `PathBuf::file_name()`), sets `is_open=1` and updates `last_used`; idempotent.  
`list_open_projects(pool: &SqlitePool) -> Result<Vec<Project>, sqlx::Error>` — returns projects where `is_open=1`, ordered by `last_used DESC`.  
`get_project(pool: &SqlitePool, project_id: i64) -> Result<Project, sqlx::Error>` — fetches single project by ID.  
`close_project(pool: &SqlitePool, project_id: i64) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error>` — sets `is_open=0` (soft close, no delete).  
`upsert_document(pool: &SqlitePool, project_id: i64, doc_type: &str, content: &str) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error>` — `INSERT OR REPLACE` into `documents` with composite PK `(project_id, doc_type)`.  
`get_documents(pool: &SqlitePool, project_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<CachedDoc>, sqlx::Error>` — returns all documents for a project.

## Schema

`projects` table columns: `id` (INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT), `folder_path` (TEXT UNIQUE), `name` (TEXT), `is_open` (INTEGER DEFAULT 1), `last_used` (TEXT DEFAULT `datetime('now')`), `created_at` (TEXT DEFAULT `datetime('now')`).  
`documents` table columns: `project_id` (INTEGER FK → `projects(id)`), `doc_type` (TEXT), `content` (TEXT DEFAULT ''), `updated_at` (TEXT DEFAULT `datetime('now')`); PK is `(project_id, doc_type)`.  
DB URL format: `sqlite:{path}?mode=rwc` (read-write-create).

## Test Conventions

Tests use `sqlite::memory:` pool with explicit schema creation in `temp_db()` helper; `#[tokio::test]` async tests; 1-second `sleep` between timestamp-sensitive inserts to ensure SQLite `datetime('now')` produces distinct values; Unix path extraction tests only (macOS/Linux servers per comment).

entries.response:
**Purpose: Orchestrates the full execution pipeline: decompose spec → scaffold pipeline → run with SSE streaming events.**

## Exports

- `ExecutionPhase` enum: `Decomposing | Scaffolding | Running`
- `ExecutionResponse` struct: `{ session_id, epic_id, pipeline_path }` — all `String` fields
- `start_execution(project_id: i64) -> Result<ExecutionResponse, ServerFnError<NoCustomError>>` — Leptos `#[server]` fn; three-phase chain (decompose → scaffold → run)
- `run_pipeline_with_streaming(graph, session_id) -> Result<PipelineResult, AttractorError>` — internal SSR-only; walks graph, resolves handlers, emits SSE events, tracks cost

## Key Internal Helpers

- `pas_cli_path() -> String` — resolves `PAS_CLI_PATH` env var, fallback `"pas"`
- `attr_to_json(attr) -> serde_json::Value` — converts `attractor_dot::AttributeValue` to JSON (supports String, Integer, Float, Boolean, Duration)
- `status_str(status) -> String` — maps `StageStatus` → `&str` ("success", "partial_success", "retry", "fail", "skipped")

## Algorithm

Three-step chain:
1. `attractor decompose {folder}/.pas/spec.md` → parse `Epic ID: attractor-xxx` from stdout
2. `attractor scaffold <epic-id>` → reads `pipelines/{epic_id}.dot`
3. Parse DOT via `attractor_dot::parse`, build `PipelineGraph`, spawn background task

Graph walker loop: `select_edge` resolves routing via `"outcome"` / `"preferred_label"` context keys; `Msquare` shape = terminal break; cost accumulated from `{node_id}.cost_usd` context keys.

## Integration

- Database: `sqlx::SqlitePool` via `use_context`, calls `crate::server::db::get_project`
- Streaming: `crate::server::stream::publish_event` for `node_start`, `node_complete`, `pipeline_complete`, `error` events
- Pipeline: `attractor_pipeline::{default_registry, select_edge, PipelineGraph, PipelineResult}`
- Types: `attractor_types::{AttractorError, Context, StageStatus}`

## SSE Event Shapes

```json
{ "type": "node_start", "node_id": "...", "label": "..." }
{ "type": "node_complete", "node_id": "...", "status": "...", "cost_usd": 0.0, "notes": "..." }
{ "type": "pipeline_complete", "total_cost_usd": 0.0, "completed_nodes": [...] }
{ "type": "error", "message": "..." }
```

## Config

- `PAS_CLI_PATH` env var for CLI override
- Project folder must contain `.pas/spec.md`
- Pipeline output path: `pipelines/{epic_id}.dot`

entries.response:
**builtin/mod.rs re-exports 6 built-in agent tools.**

## Exports
- EditFileTool - struct: replaces exact strings, supports replace_all, requires non-empty old_string
- GlobTool - struct: glob pattern matching, 20k char truncation
- GrepTool - struct: regex search, case_insensitive option, 20k truncation
- ReadFileTool - struct: reads with line numbers, offset/limit, 50k limit
- ShellTool - struct: executes commands, timeout_ms default 10000ms, 30k truncation
- WriteFileTool - struct: writes files, auto-creates parent directories

## Implementation Details
All 6 tools implement `async_trait::async_trait` Tool, depend on `ExecutionEnvironment`, use `truncation::truncate_output` with tool-specific max chars (20k-50k).

entries.response:
**Purpose**: Manages conversation history truncation strategies as context grows.

## Exported Types

`FidelityMode` enum variants: `Full` (default), `Truncate { keep_last: usize }`, `Compact`, `Summary`.

## Exported Functions

`FidelityMode::parse(s: &str) -> Self` parses string attribute values: "full", "compact", "summary", "truncate:N" (N defaults to 50), "truncate(N)". Unknown strings default to `Full`. Case-insensitive matching.

`apply_fidelity<T: Clone>(messages: &[T], mode: &FidelityMode) -> Vec<T>` applies mode to message history. Returns cloned vector (no in-place modification). `Full` returns all messages. `Truncate { keep_last }` returns last N messages, or all if length ≤ N. `Compact`/`Summary` currently return all (placeholder for future LLM integration).

## Test Coverage

Nine unit tests verify `parse` (including truncation syntax variants, case insensitivity, defaults) and `apply_fidelity` (all modes). Default is `Full`.

entries.response:
**Purpose: `stream.rs` implements SSE endpoint for real-time progress streaming via in-memory broadcast channels keyed by `session_id`.**

## Exports

- `stream_events(Path(session_id)) -> Sse<impl Stream>` — SSE handler at `/api/stream/{session_id}`; on connect sends `state_sync` event with buffered history (up to 100 events) for browser reconnect, then streams broadcast events with `KeepAlive::default()`.
- `publish_event(session_id, event)` — Fire-and-forget publish to `CHANNELS`; stores event in `SESSION_STATE` (last 100 events), silently drops if no clients connected.
- `cleanup_inactive_sessions()` — Periodic cleanup of `CHANNELS` and `SESSION_STATE` entries with zero subscribers; retains entries for active sessions.
- `clear_session_state(session_id)` — Removes session from `SESSION_STATE` after pipeline completion to free memory.

## State

- `CHANNELS: RwLock<HashMap<String, broadcast::Sender<String>>>` — In-memory broadcast channels; Phase 3 implementation; no durability across restarts.
- `SESSION_STATE: RwLock<HashMap<String, Vec<String>>>` — Event replay buffer per session; max 100 events; drained from head when exceeded.

## Key Behaviors

- `state_sync` JSON payload: `{"type": "state_sync", "events": [...], "timestamp": "<RFC3339>"}` sent on every new SSE connection if history exists.
- `BroadcastStream` filters `Err` variants to `None` (drops receiver errors silently); `Ok` text wrapped as `Event::default().data(text)`.
- `sender.receiver_count() > 0` used as activity gate for cleanup.
- All identifiers reference `session_id` strings from URL path.

## Integration

- `axum::response::sse` for SSE type; `tokio::sync::broadcast` for pub/sub; `tokio_stream::wrappers::BroadcastStream` for stream conversion.
- Phase 5 note: replace `CHANNELS`/`SESSION_STATE` with Redis/persistent broker for production.
- `chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339()` for state_sync timestamps.

entries.response:
**Purpose statement here.**

**Purpose:** Provides a WebSocket PTY bridge for embedding a Claude Code terminal with session persistence across browser refreshes and server restarts.

## Exports

`TerminalSession` struct holds `master`, `writer`, `reader`, `child`, `disconnected_at` fields — PTY process survives WebSocket disconnection.

`TerminalSessions` struct wraps `Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<TerminalSession>>>>` — thread-safe session registry with `get`, `insert`, `remove`, `reap(max_idle)` methods.

`ws_terminal(ws, Query, State)` async fn handles `WebSocketUpgrade` → `impl IntoResponse`; delegates to `handle_terminal_socket`.

`WsQuery` deserializes `?session=<id>&folder=<path>` query params.

## Session Lifecycle

1. **No `?session` param**: `spawn_claude_pty(cwd)` creates PTY, generates `uuid::Uuid::new_v4()`, stores in `TerminalSessions`, sends `{"type":"session","session_id":"..."}` as first WebSocket frame.

2. **`?session=<id>` found**: looks up existing session; if found, sets `disconnected_at = None`; if not found (server restarted), spawns fresh `claude` and issues new UUID to avoid hijacking external sessions.

3. **WebSocket disconnect**: sets `disconnected_at = Some(Instant::now())`, keeps PTY alive; `reap(30min)` runs opportunistically on each new connection via `sessions.reap(Duration::from_secs(1800))`.

## Wire Protocol

- **Connect**: `ws://host/path?session=<id>&folder=<path>`
- **First server→client**: `{"type":"session","session_id":"<uuid>"}` (JSON text frame)
- **Ongoing server→client**: binary PTY stdout (`4096` byte buffer in `spawn_blocking`)
- **Client→server**: binary keyboard input or `{"type":"resize","cols":N,"rows":N}` JSON text
- **Resize**: `master.resize(PtySize { rows, cols, pixel_width: 0, pixel_height: 0 })`

## PTY Setup

`spawn_claude_pty(cwd)` opens `native_pty_system()` with `PtySize { rows: 24, cols: 80, pixel_width: 0, pixel_height: 0 }`, spawns `CommandBuilder::new("claude")` with `cwd`, clones reader/writer from master.

## Concurrency

Two `tokio::spawn` tasks: `send_task` reads PTY stdout in `spawn_blocking` with `4096` byte buffer and sends `Message::Binary`; `recv_task` processes `Message::Binary` (→ PTY stdin) and `Message::Text` (resize JSON or plain text input). `tokio::select!` waits for both; `disconnected_at` set after completion.

entries.response:
**crates/attractor-web/src/server/projects.rs provides Leptos SSR server functions for project management and directory browsing.**

## Exported Types

- `Project` — struct with `id: i64`, `folder_path: String`, `name: String` (SSR: re-exported from `crate::server::db`, client: defined inline)
- `CachedDoc` — struct with `doc_type: String`, `content: String` (SSR: re-exported, client: inline)
- `CachedDocs` — struct with `prd: Option<String>`, `spec: Option<String>`
- `DirEntry` — struct with `name: String`, `path: String`, `is_dir: bool`

## Server Functions (Leptos #[server] macro)

- `list_open_projects() -> Result<Vec<Project>, ServerFnError>` — queries DB for open projects sorted by recency
- `open_project(folder_path: String) -> Result<Project, ServerFnError>` — validates path exists/is directory, canonicalizes, scaffolds `.pas/CLAUDE.md`, upserts to DB
- `close_project(project_id: i64) -> Result<(), ServerFnError>` — marks project as closed without deleting data
- `get_cached_documents(project_id: i64) -> Result<CachedDocs, ServerFnError>` — fetches "prd" and "spec" doc types from DB
- `list_directory(path: String) -> Result<Vec<DirEntry>, ServerFnError>` — lists subdirectories for folder picker, filters hidden dirs (`.`), adds parent `..` entry, returns sorted by name

## Internal Functions

- `scaffold_attractor_config(project_dir: &Path)` — creates `.pas/CLAUDE.md` if missing, injects PRD/spec templates from bundled files
- `load_bundled_template(filename: &str) -> Option<String>` — searches `templates/` sibling dirs and CWD for template files

## Behavioral Contracts

- `.pas/CLAUDE.md` must exist at exact path for web UI to watch PRD/spec files
- PRD file path: `.pas/prd.md` — web UI watches this exact path
- Spec file path: `.pas/spec.md` — web UI watches this exact path
- Hidden directories starting with `.` filtered from `list_directory` output
- Parent `..` entry included unless at filesystem root

## Dependencies

- `sqlx::SqlitePool` — database connection injection via `use_context()`
- `tracing` — info/warn logging for scaffold operations
- `leptos::prelude::ServerFnError` — server function error type
- `serde::{Deserialize, Serialize}` — type serialization

## Feature Gating

All DB operations, file system operations, and template loading gated behind `#[cfg(feature = "ssr")]`. Client builds get stub type definitions only.

## Annex References

- `ATTRACTOR_CLAUDE_MD_HEADER` — 40-line markdown constant defining Claude Code instructions for PRD/spec file paths and rules (reproduction-critical)

entries.response:
**crates/attractor-agent/src/lib.rs** implements the core autonomous agent loop: LLM calls + tool execution cycles coordinated by `AgentSession`.

## Exports

**Submodules** (pub mod):
- `fidelity` — exports `apply_fidelity`, `FidelityMode`
- `loop_detection` — exports `LoopDetector`, `SteeringInjector`
- `prompt_builder` — exports `discover_project_docs`, `ProjectDoc`, `SystemPromptBuilder`
- `subagent` — exports `SubagentConfig`, `SubagentManager`, `SubagentStatus`

**Constants**:
- `MAX_TOOL_OUTPUT_LEN: usize = 30_000` — truncation threshold for tool output strings

**Public Types**:
- `SessionConfig` — `{ model: String, system_prompt: String, max_turns: usize, max_tool_rounds: usize, default_command_timeout_ms: u64, enable_loop_detection: bool, loop_detection_window: usize }`; default model: `"claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"`, max_tool_rounds: 200
- `SessionState` — enum `{ Idle, Processing, AwaitingInput, Closed }`
- `Turn` — enum `{ User { content }, Assistant { content, tool_calls }, ToolResults { results }, System { content }, Steering { content } }`
- `ToolResultEntry` — `{ tool_call_id, tool_name, content, is_error }`
- `AgentSession` — coordinates LLM client, tool registry, execution environment, and history

**AgentSession Methods**:
- `new(llm_client, tool_registry, env, config) -> Self`
- `id() -> &str`
- `state() -> &SessionState`
- `history() -> &[Turn]`
- `steer(message: String)` — queues steering message for injection at next tool round boundary
- `follow_up(message: String)` — queues follow-up input for processing after current input completes
- `process_input(user_input: &str) async -> Result<String>` — core loop; processes follow-up queue after each input
- `process_single_input(user_input: &str) async -> Result<String>` — internal; enforces `max_turns` (throws `AttractorError::TurnLimitReached`), iterates tool-use loop up to `max_tool_rounds`
- `build_request() -> Request` — maps `Turn` history to `Message` list; converts `ToolRegistry` definitions to `attractor_llm::ToolDefinition`
- `execute_tool_calls(tool_calls) async -> Vec<ToolResultEntry>` — executes via `tool_registry.get()`, truncates output at `MAX_TOOL_OUTPUT_LEN` using char-boundary-safe split, formats errors as `"Error: {}"`, unknown tools as `"Unknown tool: {}"`

## Behavioral Contracts

- Truncation template: `"\n\n[WARNING: Output truncated. {} characters removed.]"` with `is_char_boundary` split
- Steering drained as `Turn::Steering` between tool rounds (before next LLM call)
- Unknown tool returns `is_error: true` with message containing `"Unknown tool"`
- `max_turns == 0` means unlimited turns
- `process_input` processes follow-up queue via `VecDeque::pop_front` loop

## Dependencies

- `attractor_llm::{ContentPart, Message, Request, ToolCallResult}` — LLM interface
- `attractor_tools::{ExecutionEnvironment, ToolRegistry}` — tool execution
- `attractor_types::AttractorError` — error types (`TurnLimitReached`)
- `uuid::Uuid` — session ID generation
- `tracing` — structured logging at info/debug levels

## Test Infrastructure

`test_utils` module provides `make_client`, `EchoTool`, `MockEnv`, `SequenceMockProvider` for mock LLM responses.
